user_intent_preservation.original_goal: Migrate the TC387 GTM TOM 3-phase inverter PWM to TC4D7 eGTM ATOM using the unified IfxEgtm_Pwm driver.
user_intent_preservation.functional_behavior: Generate 3-phase, 20 kHz center-aligned complementary PWM with 1 µs rising/falling deadtime via DTM, per-phase independent duty updates with synchronous apply on period event, and one period interrupt on CPU0 (priority 20).
user_intent_preservation.technical_approach: Use the unified IfxEgtm_Pwm on TC4D7 eGTM Cluster 1, ATOM1 CH0/1/2; enable EGTM CMU Clk1 at 100 MHz; map pins using IfxEgtm_PinMap to KIT_A3G_TC4D7_LITE macros; configure center-aligned counting and DTM deadtime through IfxEgtm_Pwm; perform duty updates using non-immediate APIs for synchronous on-period apply; install a single period ISR on CPU0.

driver_selection.selection_rationale: Unified eGTM PWM driver supports multi-channel synchronized PWM, complementary outputs with DTM deadtime, center-aligned mode, and synchronous updates — exactly matching the 3-phase inverter requirements. It is validated in the target template for TC4xx.

test_hooks: Weak-symbol HAL wrappers for IfxEgtm_Pwm, IfxEgtm_Cmu, and IfxEgtm_PinMap to enable link-time substitution with mocks
Compile-time switch EGTM_PWM_USE_MOCK to redirect hardware calls to mock backends
ISR callback injection for the period-event handler to allow host-side triggering in tests
file_structure.test_strategy: Expose a thin, mockable HAL interface so logic can be exercised on a host without eGTM hardware. Unit tests validate initialization sequencing, duty boundary checks, deadtime configuration, synchronous apply behavior, and error paths using simulation stubs for IfxEgtm_* APIs. Hardware-in-the-loop tests can optionally verify timing and deadtime via scope capture.

runtime_function.name: updateEgtmAtom3phInvDuty
runtime_function.purpose: Queue per-phase duty requests and apply synchronously on the next PWM period event.

driver_changes: GTM module reference -> EGTM module reference

api_changes.reason: TC3xx→TC4xx naming (GTM→EGTM, TOM→ATOM) and unified driver
TC3xx→TC4xx naming (GTM→EGTM, TOM→ATOM) and unified driver

migration_notes.context_preserved: The unified IfxEgtm_Pwm driver is used on TC4D7 eGTM ATOM1 channels 0/1/2 with DTM, 20 kHz center-aligned operation, 1 µs deadtime, synchronous multi-channel updates, and a single period ISR on CPU0. Clocking and pins are configured to match the KIT_A3G_TC4D7_LITE mappings.

parameterization_strategy.approach: Abstract hardware bindings into a configuration structure and board-specific macros to ease adaptation to new boards or pinouts.
parameterization_strategy.config_struct_example: EgtmPwm_Config { cluster, atom_module, channels[3], cmu_clock, cmu_frequency_hz, pin_map[] }
override_mechanisms: Preprocessor macros (e.g., IFX_CFG_EGTM_CLUSTER, IFX_CFG_EGTM_ATOM_MODULE, IFX_CFG_EGTM_CHANNELS, IFX_CFG_EGTM_PINMAP)
Board-specific header providing an EgtmPwm_Config instance (e.g., board_kit_a3g_tc4d7_lite.h)
Link-time weak defaults that can be overridden by application
parameterization_strategy.selection: Select configuration at compile time via BOARD_CFG define or include path; runtime selection optional via pointer to EgtmPwm_Config.

hardware_binding.portability_notes: Current mappings remain as defaults; alternate boards can supply their own EgtmPwm_Config and pin maps without touching module code.

notes: Use IfxEgtm_Cmu_enableClocks and IfxEgtm_Cmu_selectClkInput to enable and select Clk1 at 100 MHz.
Center-aligned mode and base/sync channel chaining are set via IfxEgtm_Pwm Config/ChannelConfig during init.
Set DTM deadtime using IfxEgtm_Pwm_updateChannelsDeadTime with 100 ticks for 1 µs at 100 MHz.
Perform per-phase changes with IfxEgtm_Pwm_updateChannelDuty or batch with IfxEgtm_Pwm_updateChannelsDuty for synchronous on-period apply.
GPIO port headers IfxPort.h / IfxPort_Pinmap.h remain unchanged.

error_and_edge_case_handling.invalid_duty_cycle: Reject and return an error for duties outside valid limits; do not apply the update. Valid range enforces non-overlap with deadtime (e.g., min/max clamped so 2*deadtime <= period).
error_and_edge_case_handling.deadtime_constraints: Validate deadtime against period during init and updates; if invalid, fail configuration with an error and keep outputs disabled.
error_and_edge_case_handling.clock_failures: Verify CMU Clk1 availability/frequency at init; on failure or drift detection, stop PWM outputs and report a hardware fault.
error_and_edge_case_handling.pin_conflicts: Detect pin allocation conflicts before enabling outputs; on conflict, abort init and leave pins in safe state.
error_and_edge_case_handling.mode_conflicts: If an immediate update is requested while synchronous mode is enabled (or vice versa), return a mode-conflict error without changing outputs.
error_and_edge_case_handling.diagnostics: Provide optional user callback for error reporting and counters for missed period interrupts or late updates.
error_and_edge_case_handling.safe_state: On critical errors, force complementary outputs to inactive state and stop synchronized channels to prevent shoot-through.
